artworks.title: Starry Night Over the Rhône
artworks.artistBio: Vincent van Gogh (1853-1890) was a Dutch Post-Impressionist painter who created about 2,100 artworks in just over a decade.
artworks.description: Van Gogh painted this view of the Rhône river at night. The stars were captured in the cobalt blue sky, and the gaslights of Arles reflected in the water. This was one of his first night scenes, showing his fascination with depicting night effects.

artworks.title: The Persistence of Memory
artworks.artistBio: Salvador Dalí (1904-1989) was a Spanish surrealist artist renowned for his technical skill and striking imagery.
artworks.description: The Persistence of Memory is a surrealist masterpiece featuring melting clocks in a dreamlike landscape. It explores the concept of time as fluid and non-linear.
artworks.venue: Museum of Modern Art, New York, USA

artworks.title: Girl with a Pearl Earring
artworks.artistBio: Johannes Vermeer (1632-1675) was a Dutch Baroque painter who specialized in domestic interior scenes.
artworks.description: Girl with a Pearl Earring is often called the 'Mona Lisa of the North'. It depicts a young girl wearing an exotic turban and a large pearl earring.

artworks.title: The Two Fridas
artworks.artistBio: Frida Kahlo (1907-1954) was a Mexican painter known for her many portraits and works inspired by Mexican culture.
artworks.description: The Two Fridas is a double self-portrait depicting two versions of Kahlo sitting together. One is dressed in European attire, the other in traditional Tehuana costume.
artworks.venue: Museo de Arte Moderno, Mexico City, Mexico

announcements.title: Summer Exhibition Opening
announcements.description: Join us for the grand opening of our summer collection featuring works from emerging artists.

announcements.title: Artist Spotlight: Vincent van Gogh
announcements.description: Explore the life and works of one of history's most influential artists.
